schemas.description: Shared Japanese collection schema types for reusable sentence-unit based content models.
Shared Spanish collection schema types for reusable sentence and word content models.

collections.name: Periodic Table – First 70 Elements
collections.description: Atomic number, symbol, and core properties for the first 70 elements of the periodic table.

collections.name: Pokémon Starters (Kanto)
collections.description: First-generation Kanto starter Pokémon and first 151 Pokémon. Pokémon `type` values use the official Japanese main-series game type labels (e.g., ノーマル, ほのお, みず, でんき, くさ, どく, ひこう, etc.). Name root fields capture both surface segmentation and etymology for Japanese and English names.

collections.name: Programming
collections.description: Consolidated programming collections: languages, SQL engines, web technologies.

collections.name: Greek Alphabet
collections.description: Greek letters with standard alphabetical order, lowercase/uppercase forms, classical names, letter type classification, and pronunciation guides split into Classical vs Modern (IPA + simple helpers).

collections.name: Greek Mythology
collections.description: Consolidated Greek mythology entries (Olympians, Titans, Primordials, Chthonic).

collections.name: Japanese Grammar
collections.description: Consolidated Japanese grammar patterns with quick examples and links to sentence and paragraph usage.

collections.name: Japanese Paragraphs
collections.description: Learner-friendly multi-sentence Japanese reading passages with sentence-level chunking.

collections.name: Japanese Sentences
collections.description: Ichidan verbs with counters, proper nouns, and culturally useful notes.

collections.name: Japanese Songs
collections.description: Song lyrics with learner-friendly sentence-level analysis and chunks.

collections.name: All Japanese Words
collections.description: First-generation Japanese words from various sources including dictionaries and language databases.

entry.meaning: to play; to hang out; to have fun
to fly; to jump; to go fast (e.g., time passes quickly)
to wake up; to get up
to be able to; can do; to be made; to be completed
to come off; to be removed; to be obtained/caught
tough; hard; serious; very (adverbial use also common)

collections.name: Spanish Sentences
collections.description: Spanish learner sentences with English translations and chunk references into the Spanish words collection.

collections.name: All Spanish Words
collections.description: First-generation Spanish vocabulary entries as learner-useful terms, with English glosses and lightweight grammatical metadata.
